behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-handoff wiring (taxonomy source -> request builder -> API client), not pure local logic. No real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this; a captured in-process client request is sufficient. It does not depend on model output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound payload. Invoke classify with a taxonomy and assert every valid label appears in the sent request, with no dropped labels across representative taxonomies.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is response-handoff validation (API response -> normalization -> module output). A unit test of normalization alone may miss wiring bugs where raw provider value is leaked. Real infra is unnecessary; a stubbed provider response reproduces the failure. No dependence on real model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API adapter returning an out-of-taxonomy label (for example "medical"). Call classify and assert returned value is exactly "unknown" and never the raw provider label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text scopes to normalization determinism for identical raw input, which is a single-function/property check with no module boundary required. Higher levels are disqualified because no real infra or model call is needed once raw response is fixed.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert output label is identical on every run and has no hidden state dependence.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI calls and consistency of returned labels, which depends on real model/provider behavior. Mocking would hide model-side variability and cannot validate this failure mode. Workflow is disqualified because this is a single agentic step repeated, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications for the same document/taxonomy against the real provider, record raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label consistency whenever raw provider response is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff/order boundary: budget enforcement must happen before API dispatch. A pure unit test may miss accidental dispatch despite policy. Real OS/network not needed; fake client can assert dispatch/no-dispatch and payload mutation. No dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a low token budget and fake API client. Submit oversized document and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) and that dispatch behavior matches policy (truncated payload sent or call blocked).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and real provider-reported usage; mocks can trivially pass and hide real tokenization/accounting behavior. Because execution path depends on a real AI API call, keep Agentic (not Integration). Workflow is disqualified: single call/measurement, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative documents using the production model settings, read provider usage metadata, and assert reported total tokens do not exceed configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration across modules (error mapping -> wait policy -> retry call -> final return). In-process stubs can reproduce rate-limit responses and verify retry behavior; real network/OS is not required for the core failure. No model-output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns rate-limit error on first call and success on second, with controlled retry-after metadata. Assert module waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
